recalls.Description: Goal Zero has announced a recall of its Yeti Link Expansion Modules and EC8 power cables due to potential fire and burn hazards. These products were sold both as kits and individually, intended for use in homes and vehicles. In vehicles, these modules connect to the alternator for charging the battery while the engine runs, while in homes, they facilitate the addition of batteries to the Yeti Power Station/Generator. The recalled modules are identified by model numbers 98200 and 98210, with Goal Zero branding on them. The associated cables include various models such as 98203, 98204, and others.
recalls.Title: Goal Zero Issues Recall for Yeti Link Expansion Modules and EC8 Cables Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Goal Zero at 888-794-6250 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email support@goalzero.com, or visit www.goalzero.com and click on 'Notices/Recalls' in the Support section for further details.

Products.Name: Yeti Link Expansion Module
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 34,460 (In addition, about 815 were sold in Canada) (The EC8 cables were previously recalled in April 2021)

Images.Caption: Recalled Male EC8 to Ring Cable
Recalled Female EC8 to Ring Cable
Recalled MPPTx to Tank Cable with EC8 Connector

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the cables melting inside a vehicle, causing minor property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Importers.Name: Goal Zero LLC, of Draper, Utah

Hazards.Name: Overheating and melting connections leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled products and request a free replacement kit from Goal Zero

recalls.metaDescription: Goal Zero recalls Yeti Link Modules and EC8 Cables due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement kit.
